key_insight: Zero-field phonon Hall viscosity can act as a symmetry-sensitive fingerprint of altermagnetism, clearly separating it from ferromagnets and conventional antiferromagnets.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical magneto-acoustic route to detect broken symmetries and topological features in insulating altermagnets through strain-response measurements.
method: Formulated the Hall viscosity tensor for phonons in altermagnets.
Computed Hall viscosity microscopically for d-wave and g-wave altermagnet models.
Connected strong viscosity signatures to gapped Dirac points and Lifshitz transitions via strain-space Berry curvature.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical and microscopic model calculations showing that specific Hall-viscosity tensor components unambiguously distinguish magnetic classes and are highly sensitive to electronic spectral features.
watch_item: Experimental use may be limited by the need to resolve subtle magneto-acoustic signals and by material-specific sensitivity to band-structure details.
